title: Sort and Defend
main_student_action: Sort items into categories and defend the choice with word-part logic.
what_it_is: A classification routine where students place words or word parts into categories, then defend why the sort makes sense.
why_it_stays_in_bank: It slows down the thinking just enough to reveal misconceptions without losing game energy.
non_negotiable_game_rule: Students cannot score from a sort alone; they must defend the move with visible evidence.
teacher_script: Sort first, then prove the sort.
If the sort is arguable, your explanation has to carry the point.
student_job: Place the item, defend the choice, and revise if the explanation does not hold.
required_literacy_protections: categories must map to real meaning or form logic
teacher must resolve arguable items explicitly
harder_version: require a second example from the same category
good_teacher_language: If you can defend it, keep it there.
extension_questions: Which item was hardest to place and why?
exit_slip_options: Write one category and one item that clearly belongs there.
